codeReviews.title: Code reviews
codeReviews.descMulti: Automatic reviews of PRs from {n} workspace sources
codeReviews.descConnect: Connect a GitHub repo to the workspace to review PRs here
codeReviews.noGithubSource: Connect a GitHub repository to view and review Pull Requests here.
codeReviews.noPrHereHint: When you open a Pull Request in the connected repositories, it shows up here.
codeReviews.noAgentConfigured: No agent with a configured adapter in this workspace.
codeReviews.reanalyzeHint: Click "Re-analyze" to include these PRs in the analysis
codeReviews.linkPrsDesc: Linked PRs are analyzed together (e.g. back + front). The agent evaluates the set, finding cross-stack bugs and broken contracts.
codeReviews.searchLinkPlaceholder: Search by title, number, repo or author…
codeReviews.author: Author
codeReviews.noReposLinked: This workspace has no linked GitHub repos. Add sources in the sidebar to link cross-repo PRs.
codeReviews.noPrWithFilter: No PRs found with this filter.
codeReviews.noAgentsDesc: To run code reviews you need at least ONE agent with a configured adapter (Claude / Codex / Gemini). Create an agent in the sidebar (+ next to AGENTS) and configure the adapter in the agent's "Configuration" tab.
codeReviews.multiPrSubOne: {n} PR evaluated together with the main one
codeReviews.multiPrSubMany: {n} PRs evaluated together with the main one
codeReviews.noInlineComments: No inline comments. The reviewer found no issues worth flagging.
codeReviews.emptyReviewDesc: Click "Analyze now" to have {who} generate a full analysis of this PR's diff — rating, walkthrough, bugs, suggestions, security, performance.
codeReviews.tip1: Large PRs can take 30s to 2min — Claude/Codex process the entire diff.
codeReviews.tip2: The CLI buffers stdout until the end — don't worry if nothing shows up here.
codeReviews.tip3: The agent is reading every file, identifying bugs, suggestions and risks.
codeReviews.tip4: You can cancel anytime by clicking the "Cancel" button in the header.
codeReviews.tip5: The next run will overwrite this one — history is saved in the DB.
codeReviews.workspaceNoGithub: Workspace with no linked GitHub source
codeReviews.noGithubSourceDetail: This workspace has no GitHub source. Add a source in the sidebar.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: Connect external tools for Orkestral to use.

github.name: GitHub
github.description: Issues, PRs, repos and code review via the GitHub API.

slack.name: Slack
slack.description: Notifications and commands in the channel.

voice.name: Local transcription (Whisper)
voice.description: Transcribes audio locally with Whisper (~575 MB). Works offline, no audio sent to the cloud.

forge.name: Orkestral Forge
forge.description: Local code model, $0 per task. Runs on your machine and improves with community usage.

embeddings.name: Local embeddings
embeddings.description: Local embeddings model (~640 MB) that powers semantic code search & the knowledge base. Required, runs offline.

fastApply.name: Fast-Apply
fastApply.description: Local model (~986 MB) specialized in applying code edits — your own "morph". Kills anchor-match failures; auto-installs the first time it's needed.

sentry.name: Sentry
sentry.description: Monitor application errors and exceptions in production.
sentry.connectHint: Paste a Sentry Auth Token (Settings → Account → API → Auth Tokens) with project:read and event:read. Project is optional — empty = all projects the token can access.

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Configure AI providers (CLI or API key) and local models.
providers.cliOnly: This provider uses the CLI only (login/keychain).

forge.description: Local code model, $0 per task. Pick the variant and download.

mcps.title: MCPs
mcps.subtitle: MCP server marketplace — install and manage per agent model.

channels.title: Channels
channels.subtitle: Talk to Orkestral from messaging apps.

desc.whatsapp: Chat with your agent from your phone via WhatsApp.
desc.telegram: Chat with your agent on Telegram via a bot.
desc.slack: Messages and commands in your channel.
desc.discord: Chat with your agent in a server.
desc.msteams: Chat with your agent through Microsoft Teams.

telegram.tokenHint: Create a bot with @BotFather on Telegram and paste the token here.
telegram.tokenSaved: Token saved — leave blank to keep
telegram.allowlistHint: Leave empty to reply to anyone, or restrict to these Telegram user IDs.
telegram.invalidToken: Invalid token — check the token in @BotFather and paste it again.

signal.qrHint: In Signal on your phone: Settings → Linked devices → Link new device, then scan.

modal.configHint: Pick the agent that replies to messages from this channel.
modal.tokenHint: Paste the Discord bot token (Developer Portal → Bot → Reset Token). Enable the *Message Content* intent.
modal.tokenSaved: Token saved — paste a new one to replace
modal.teamsHint: Create the app automatically below, or register the bot in Azure and paste the App ID, client secret and Tenant ID. The app handles the tunnel and endpoint for you.
modal.teamsSaved: Saved — fill in again to replace
modal.teamsEndpointHint: The app creates and exposes the tunnel automatically. This is the public URL in use now (it changes each connection and is re-pointed for you).
modal.teamsCreateHint: We set up a secure tunnel automatically and create the app, bot and credentials for you. Requires signing in to your Microsoft account.
modal.teamsCreating: Setting up the tunnel and creating the app… this can take a minute.
modal.teamsCreated: App created! Credentials filled in — now just connect.
modal.teamsFinishLogin: Finish signing in on the browser, then click Create app.
modal.teamsDeviceInstruction: Click Open sign-in page, enter this code at login.microsoft.com/device and finish signing in. Then come back and click Create app.
modal.teamsAccountWarning: Use a work or school account (Microsoft 365). Personal accounts (outlook, hotmail, live) can't create Teams bots.
modal.allowlistHint: Leave empty to answer everyone, or restrict to these numbers.
modal.allowlistHintTeams: Leave empty to answer everyone, or restrict to these users (AAD object ID or email).
modal.allowlistEmpty: Empty — answers any number that messages you.
modal.qrHint: On your phone: WhatsApp → Linked devices → Link a device, then scan.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Reusable capabilities for agents.
skills.kindInstructionDesc: Markdown block injected into the agent's prompt
skills.kindMcpDesc: MCP server configuration the agent connects to
skills.emptyHintNew: Create the first one or install from the Marketplace.
skills.deleteConfirm: Delete the skill "{name}"? It will be removed from all agents.
skills.createDesc: Reusable capabilities that can be attached to multiple agents.
skills.skillDescPlaceholder: One line summarizing what this skill does…

logs.title: Logs
logs.description: Live execution trace · agents and chat

econ.title: Savings · Forge + Morph vs premium
econ.externalLimits: External limits: Codex/Claude expose quota via account, banner, or gateway; here we measure runs and locally avoided tokens.
econ.routingTraceHint: Use the Hybrid filter to see Forge → premium CLI → Forge in real time.

forgeRuns.title: Forge runs
forgeRuns.emptyHint: Assign a task to an executor agent to see Morph in action.

logs.restartTitle: Restart the app to enable Logs
logs.restartDesc1: The logs channel was just added. Close and reopen Orkestral (or restart
logs.noEventsHint: Assign a task to an executor agent to see the trace live.
logs.subtitle: Execution trace of agents and chat in real time.

rail.localSuccessSub: of orchestrated runs resolved without premium

costs.title: Costs
costs.description: Workspace usage and metrics (token tracking comes in the next phase)
costs.savingsDetail: {local} local resolutions × {cost} measured average premium cost per escalation. Premium spent so far: {spent}.
costs.savingsNoData: Not enough data yet: no premium escalation has a measured cost, so we can't compute savings honestly. Local resolutions: {local}.
costs.comingSoonDesc: Token tracking, cost per model and per-agent budgets come in the next phase. For now, this screen shows only aggregate counts.
costs.localPhasesDetail: {runs} analysis/classification task(s) resolved locally by Forge, at no API cost.

goals.title: Goals
goals.noGoalsDesc: Define strategic goals to guide the orchestrator and the agents.
goals.verifyHint: All issues are done — the CEO checks whether the goal was achieved
goals.planHint: The CEO breaks this goal into issues and distributes them to the team
goals.deleteBody: Linked issues will be unlinked (not deleted). This can't be undone.

goalDetail.noIssuesYet: No issues yet — ask the CEO to plan to see progress here.
goalDetail.noIssues: No issues yet. Click "Ask the CEO to plan" to break down the goal.
goalDetail.noSubGoals: Break this goal into smaller ones, if you'd like.

routines.title: Routines
routines.description: Tasks that run periodically — heartbeat with a custom prompt
routines.noRoutines: No routines — create the first one.
routines.selectOrCreate: Select a routine or create a new one
routines.defaultPrompt: Check the project state and produce a short summary.
routines.namePlaceholder: e.g. Daily summary, Weekly code review
routines.promptHint: This text is sent to the agent on every routine run.
routines.enabledDesc: The routine will be triggered automatically at the configured interval.
routines.disabledDesc: The routine only runs when you click Run now.

activity.title: Activity
activity.noActivityDesc: Create issues, switch agents, connect skills — every action shows up here.

marketplace.registryHint: Results from the Orkestral catalog + live PulseMCP registry.
marketplace.pricingTitleFreemium: Has a free tier, but requires an account/API key
marketplace.emptyInstalledMcp: Open the Marketplace and install an MCP server to get started.
marketplace.emptyInstalledSkill: Open the Marketplace and install a skill to get started.
marketplace.detectedDesc: MCPs already configured in Claude, Codex, Gemini or Cursor. Import them to manage per model here in Orkestral.
marketplace.importedToastDesc: You now manage it per model in Orkestral.
marketplace.modelScopeDesc: Installation is per model. Keep "All models" so the MCP keeps working when you switch the agent between Codex, Claude and others.
marketplace.footerEditMcp: Edit credentials or models and save.
marketplace.footerFillCreds: Fill in the required credentials to install.
marketplace.allModelsDesc: Works on any agent, even when switching from Codex to Claude.
marketplace.noAgentsUseAll: No agents in the workspace yet — use "All models".
marketplace.assignSubtitleSkill: Choose which agents will use this skill.
marketplace.assignSubtitleMcp: Choose which agents will use this MCP. Agents running the same model turn on together.
marketplace.assignSiblingNote: Agents on the same model are linked and toggle together.
marketplace.assignEmpty: No agents in this workspace yet.

sentryErrors.title: Sentry errors
sentryErrors.subtitle: Connect Sentry in Integrations to see errors here.
sentryErrors.notConnectedHint: Connect your Sentry org to view and fix errors right here.
sentryErrors.emptyHint: Your base is clean for the period. Check back later.
sentryErrors.searchPlaceholder: Search by title, file or ID…

detail.noEvent: No detailed event available for this error.

sentryAutomation.title: Sentry automation
sentryAutomation.subtitle: Watch new issues and act automatically.
sentryAutomation.enableHint: When a new issue matches the conditions, Orkestral acts on its own. Only applies to issues that appear after you enable it.
sentryAutomation.minLevelHint: Only triggers on issues at this level or higher.
sentryAutomation.modeHint: Propose lets you approve before the agent starts.
sentryAutomation.refreshHint: How often the screen and notifications refresh.

sentryAutomations.title: Sentry automations
sentryAutomations.subtitle: Rules that watch new errors and act on their own.
sentryAutomations.noRulesHint: Create a rule to watch new errors and propose or analyze automatically.
sentryAutomations.noRuns: No runs yet. When a rule acts, it shows up here.
sentryAutomations.name: Name
sentryAutomations.modeHint: Propose lets you approve before the agent starts.
